title: hibbertia stricta
intro: Hibbertia stricta, also known as Guinea Flower or Yellow Buttons, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its yellow, button-like flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Hibbertia stricta include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Hibbertia stricta is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in infusions or dried for later use.
